title: EFT: Evocative Unfolding
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: a puzzling reaction point
slides.body: Use when the client does not know why they reacted that way.
bullets: Choose one concrete moment, not a general pattern.
Shift into present tense and sensory detail.
Let implicit meaning emerge before offering formulation.

slides.title: Micro-sequence
slides.body: Experienced clinicians often move too quickly to the scheme. Stay with the client at the frame-by-frame level.
steps: Locate the scene: where, who, distance, light, posture.
Freeze the first body shift before the named emotion.
Ask: what did your system see, assume, or expect right there?
Differentiate primary reaction from secondary management.
Reflect the discovered meaning and check for bodily recognition.

slides.title: Common errors
rows: Return to one second in one scene.
Ask for body, gaze, impulse, and felt meaning.
Hold formulation lightly and test it experientially.
